title: indigofera trita subsp. scabra
intro: Indigofera trita subsp. scabra, also known as Rough Indian Shot, is a perennial legume plant native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Indigofera trita subsp. scabra include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
